Inc. reports that founders are being forced to operationalize AI risk because buyers now expect production controls instead of presentation-ready prototypes.

Inc. Premium

Teams are being asked how they handle model drift, when a human can intervene, and how quickly a risky workflow can be rolled back without freezing the whole product.

Recommended Reading

That pressure is turning approval paths, auditability, and fallback logic into practical go-to-market requirements for AI startups.
